jokes.text: Dave chappelle, going once... Dave chappelle, going twice... Fuck it. Ladies and gentlemen... Put your hands together for donnell rawlings and Charlie Murphy!
jokes.setup: Traditional formal introduction building suspense for the star
jokes.punchline: Auction-style countdown followed by immediate abandonment and substitute hosts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on Dave's departure using auction format. Perfect economy and unexpected structure.

jokes.text: But I want y'all to know somethin'. Charlie Murphy is not mad at Dave chappelle. 'Cause I happen to know if it wasn't for Dave chappelle, you motherfuckers would still be calling me Eddie Murphy's brother.
jokes.punchline: Reveals the humiliating reality that Dave gave him his own identity
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - only Charlie Murphy could deliver this line about his own fame struggles

jokes.text: Nigga, speak for yourself. I need all the camera time I can get, nigga! I'm goin' ass to the glass! Take a picture of me, son! Strike a pose!
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to speak for both of them
jokes.punchline: Donnell's desperate hunger for fame breaking through any pretense of dignity
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment with physical comedy implied. 'Ass to the glass' is memorable phrasing

jokes.text: Your chalkboard says $8, man. That's the old price.
jokes.setup: Dave pointing out the posted price
jokes.punchline: Barber's shameless price gouging the instant he knows Dave is rich
jokes.explanation: Efficient setup of the price-gouging premise. Simple but effective structure

jokes.text: All right. But for $11,000... Shave my balls then, nigga. Come on, get to cuttin'.
jokes.punchline: Demanding proportionally outrageous service for the inflated cost
jokes.explanation: Perfectly logical escalation that's both shocking and reasonable given the setup

jokes.text: $873. Aw, come on, man, the sign say $28. That's the old price.
jokes.setup: Second instance of posted vs. quoted price
jokes.punchline: Even more extreme price gouging with the exact same excuse
jokes.explanation: Effective running gag escalation but relies heavily on repetition of previous joke

jokes.text: You think that's the first time I had a gun put in my face? Nigga, that's the sixth time this has happened this week.
jokes.setup: Mechanic pulling a gun for payment
jokes.punchline: Dave's casual acceptance revealing this is a regular occurrence
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing how fame money creates absurd daily situations

jokes.text: I pay you $4,000 a week to hold me down, nigga. Hold me down!
jokes.punchline: The bodyguard is completely ineffective when actually needed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the money problems theme, showing even expensive solutions fail

jokes.text: The irs pulled the trigger, but your greed did this to me, Dave.
jokes.punchline: Blaming Dave's money/fame for creating the dangerous situation
jokes.explanation: Dark twist that adds thematic depth beyond just slapstick, meta-commentary on fame

jokes.text: I'm broke, bitch!
jokes.punchline: Donnell's version is the opposite - financial desperation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character consistency - Donnell's desperation continues

jokes.text: First he put my nuts on the dresser. It was crazy--just my nuts. And then he banged them shits with a spiked bat, like 'bow!'
jokes.punchline: Impossibly specific and graphic description of bizarre torture
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific absurdist imagery that became iconic. 'Just my nuts' detail is perfect

jokes.text: And then, he sewed my asshole shut. Kept feeding me and feeding me and feeding me!
jokes.punchline: Even more impossible and disturbing escalation
jokes.explanation: Grotesque escalation that's so absurd it becomes funny. Perfectly committed delivery implied

jokes.text: method man put a hanger on the stove and left it for, like, 40 minutes and then he stuck it in his ass real slow. Like, 'sss...'
jokes.punchline: Impossibly detailed timing and sound effects
jokes.explanation: The specific '40 minutes' and sound effect show commitment to the absurd premise

jokes.text: You know, David, I've been so proud of you all these years, and I want you to know that I'm really sorry about what happened between us. I mean, for Pete's sake, David, we were all just kids.
jokes.punchline: Setting up Dave as the victim when she clearly betrayed him
jokes.explanation: Standard setup line - necessary for the bigger payoff but not inherently funny

jokes.text: This my wife and my son! Girl, I didn't get no divorce-- we're happy as hell!
jokes.setup: Dave supposedly bringing his new life to show Shonda
jokes.punchline: Revealing the entire romantic reunion was an elaborate revenge prank
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection payoff that recontextualizes the entire previous scene

jokes.text: 'Cause I'm petty and lucky for me, my family's petty too.
jokes.setup: Shonda asking why he would do this
jokes.punchline: Dave's completely honest self-assessment and family dynamic
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and honesty - admitting to character flaws while celebrating them

jokes.text: Nice shoes. Oh! Hold on, hold on. Look at that squirrel skin coat. Oh!
jokes.setup: Family joining in on mocking Shonda
jokes.punchline: Progressively more specific and cruel fashion critiques
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the family's pettiness matching Dave's character

jokes.text: How many squirrels must die for you to look fly?
jokes.punchline: Rhyming critique that sounds like an activist slogan
jokes.explanation: Clever rhyme that elevates the basic insult into something more memorable

jokes.text: I'm rich, bitch!
jokes.punchline: Catchphrase delivery emphasizing his financial superiority
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase delivery - simple but perfectly timed and quotable

jokes.text: She sucks ass! Eight years ago at an audition, Jerry had the nerve to tell me that I didn't have, and I'm quoting her, 'it.'
jokes.punchline: Brutal public assassination of her professional reputation
jokes.explanation: Good structure using fake endorsement format to destroy someone's career

jokes.text: One time Jerry called me a 'nigga' on the set of jungle fever. Kiss my black ass, Jerry. Two times!
jokes.punchline: Adding racism allegations and then correcting the count
jokes.explanation: The 'two times' correction adds absurd specificity to the revenge fantasy

jokes.text: You can see more of Jerry in this month's cameltoe magazine.
jokes.punchline: Adding sexual humiliation to the professional destruction
jokes.explanation: Crude escalation that feels somewhat cheap compared to the career-focused revenge

jokes.text: You don't smell that gas? I was gonna burn your club down.
jokes.setup: Dave's emotional reconciliation with the club owner
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing he came prepared for arson
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark twist that recontextualizes his apparent forgiveness as calculated revenge

jokes.text: Thank you for making this that much sweeter, you son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Steve's heartfelt apology making Dave emotional
jokes.punchline: Dave using the emotional moment to make his cruelty more satisfying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant psychological twist - using genuine emotion as fuel for revenge rather than forgiveness

jokes.text: Oh, I love it when a plan comes together.
jokes.setup: Successfully executing revenge on the wheelchair-bound club owner
jokes.punchline: A-Team catchphrase delivered after cruel act
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference feels lazy compared to the more original revenge concept

jokes.text: That little motherfucker, bye!
jokes.punchline: Casually tossing/dropping the baby
jokes.explanation: Dark physical comedy but feels somewhat arbitrary shock value

jokes.text: I saw Tupac on 42nd street selling biggie t-shirts two for $10, man.
jokes.setup: Conspiracy theories about Tupac being alive
jokes.punchline: Specific, ironic image of Tupac profiting off his rival's merchandise
jokes.explanation: Clever specific detail that makes the conspiracy theory more absurd and funny

jokes.text: what can a nigga do when half the people voted for George w. / bouncin' checks from George w. / can't be true / better choke him 'cause he's a snitch / I'm talking about George w. Smith from city council / he ran in '93 out in Oakland / you probably didn't hear about him
jokes.setup: Seeming political commentary about George W. Bush
jokes.punchline: Elaborate misdirection to completely obscure local politician
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that builds up political relevance then deflates to mundane local politics

jokes.text: how am I doing this?
jokes.setup: Tupac continuing to rap despite being dead
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to acknowledge the logical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta moment where the premise becomes self-aware

jokes.text: how am I doing this?
jokes.setup: Tupac continuing to rap from beyond the grave
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall to question his own existence
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-comedy moment that acknowledges the absurdity of the premise. Maximum economy with huge impact.

jokes.text: the girl in the miniskirt has bad taste / 'cause that shirt don't match / there's a pudding stain on the back / what the fuck is that? / it might be doo-doo
jokes.punchline: Progressively more specific and gross observations about someone's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from fashion critique to hygiene horror

jokes.text: and you in the back, you ain't shit / you bought a gin and tonic, but you didn't even tip / and if you hit this table one more time / then the record might skip
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging he's on a record that could skip
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-awareness of the recording medium while maintaining character

jokes.text: Dave chappelle / that ain't your wife / damn, man, go back to your kids
jokes.punchline: Ghost rapper giving life advice to living comedian
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta moment connecting the sketch to Dave's real-life situation

jokes.text: way before Dave chappelle had two kids
jokes.punchline: Including personal details about Dave's life that a dead rapper couldn't know
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the meta-commentary about Dave's personal life

jokes.text: okay, I will
jokes.punchline: Tupac politely agreeing to his own death request
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and absurd politeness. The ultimate deadpan response to being told to rest in peace

jokes.text: I'm rich, biatch!
jokes.punchline: Final delivery of his signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase delivery for the close, though repetitive by this point
